data.body: PASS-2 verification note (independent): Confirmed via code grounding that batch_dispatch.update_one (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py:550-551) skips link adds already present in either direction, which can yield applied==0 with no failure. NOTE for finding re: dedup-skip FP — the outbound differ (outbound_differ.py:1028-1029) already excludes links present in the diff-time Jira snapshot, so a steady-state re-sync normally yields computed==0 (no canary trip). The applied==0/computed>0 divergence is confined to the narrow documented race (re-fetched get_issue_links at :536 finding a link the differ saw as absent). The distinction between failed-no-op and dedup-skipped-no-op is genuinely not addressed in the plan/AC, but the false-positive frequency is narrower than a blanket "on a re-sync" claim implies.
